title: Migrate Morty roles to native Claude Code subagent definitions
source: Claude Code subagents (.claude/agents/*.md with name/description/tools/model frontmatter) — knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; verify at docs.claude.com/claude-code
description: The system hand-rolls agent delegation via `claude -p` subprocesses. Claude Code now supports first-class subagent definitions in `.claude/agents/` with per-agent tool allowlists, model overrides, and isolated context. Defining each Morty role (reviewer, tester, implementer, security/Unity) as a native subagent gives per-role least-privilege tool scoping and lets the orchestrator invoke them by type, reducing custom subprocess plumbing and improving safety.
proposed_changes: Create `.claude/agents/<role>.md` files for each Morty role with frontmatter (name, description, tools allowlist scoped to what the role needs, model). Update team.py/delegate.py to invoke the matching subagent type and restrict its tools via the definition rather than passing full permissions. Keep the existing subprocess path as a fallback behind a config flag while validating parity on a low-risk ticket first.
